data.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Did Not Disappoint. It Just Missed a High Bar
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) did not completely disappoint on Q2 results. It just failed to clear an exceptionally high bar. The Q3 artificial intelligence (AI) semiconductor revenue guidance came in slightly below consensus expectations, triggering a sharp sell-off of more than 20% from the stock’s recent all-time high (ATH) of $495. Yet guidance missing a high bar is not the same as AI demand weakening, and Broadcom’s Q2 earnings confirm that the AI infrastructure supercycle is intact.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

I remain bullish on AVGO because record revenue and free cash flow, along with $30 billion in AI bookings, mean the long-term AI growth outlook remains firmly on track.
data.author: Nurhan Gecgil

data.title: Top Morgan Stanley Analyst Lifts UnitedHealth (UNH) Stock Price Target amid Rising Optimism
data.description:
UnitedHealth (UNH) stock surged 5.2% on Thursday amid growing optimism about the health insurer’s improving prospects. Notably, Bank of America analyst Kevin Fischbeck upgraded his rating on UNH stock to Buy from Hold and raised his price target to $450 from $420, citing improving medical cost trends and supportive near-term data points. Additionally, top Morgan Stanley analyst Erin Wright reaffirmed a Buy rating on UnitedHealth stock and boosted her price target to $453 from $395. Let’s look at the reasons driving Wright’s higher price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Here’s Why Morgan Stanley Analyst Is Bullish on UNH Stock  

Wright noted that stocks of managed care organizations (MCOs) have been trending higher amid indications of softer utilization trends. As of Thursday’s closing, Wright’s revised price target of $453 implies 14.3% upside potential for UNH stock from current levels.

Interestingly, the 5-star analyst expects MCOs’ revenues and costs to benefit from artificial intelligence (AI)-driven efficiencies. Wright contends that the market has not yet priced in the impact of these AI tailwinds into the valuations of MCO stocks. The analyst expects AI tailwinds to drive about a 45% average upside in MCOs’ earnings per share (EPS) as efficiencies scale.

UNH stock has risen 20% year-to-date. The company delivered better-than-expected Q1 results and raised its full-year adjusted earnings guidance, indicating it is better positioned to manage high medical costs. Also, UnitedHealth announced a 5% hike in its quarterly dividend to $2.32 per share. UNH stock currently offers a yield of about 3%.

Is UNH Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Overall, Wall Street is bullish on UnitedHealth’s prospects and has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 19 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average UNH stock price target of $403.09 indicates a modest upside potential of 1.7% from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: UnitedHealth Raises Dividend by 5% as Turnaround Efforts Continue
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group (UNH) has raised its quarterly dividend by 5% to $2.32 per share following its annual shareholder meeting. This marks the company’s 17th consecutive annual dividend increase. The move comes as UnitedHealth works through a long turnaround and looks to improve its core insurance and Optum services businesses.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The higher dividend shows management’s confidence in its near-term performance, even as the company deals with higher medical costs, pressure in Medicare Advantage, and ongoing changes inside Optum. The payout will go to shareholders of record on June 15, 2026, and will be paid on June 23, 2026.

The increase comes at a key moment for the health insurance giant. UnitedHealth has been cutting costs, reshaping its clinic network, and tightening operations after a tough stretch marked by weaker profits and more regulatory scrutiny. It has also been trying to restore investor trust after operational issues and membership losses in its insurance unit.

Raising the dividend signals that the company sees improving cash flow and a more stable path ahead. It also fits with UnitedHealth’s long record of steady dividend growth, a key reason for appealing long‑term investors.

Is UNH Stock a Buy or Sell?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on UnitedHealth stock based on 18 Buy, four Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average UNH stock price target of $399.09 indicates 5.86% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Kohl’s Stock (KSS) Goes Ballistic on Earnings Beat and Stronger Outlook
data.description:
Shares of Kohl’s (KSS) exploded sharply on Thursday after the U.S. retailer revealed its quarterly earnings beat Wall Street forecasts despite low sales. The management also said sales momentum is starting to improve as the firm works to steady operations and win back its market share.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Kohl’s Stock Fires Up on Earnings Beat and Improving Sales

The price of KSS climbed more than 17% in a single day after the company reported solid earnings. The stock is now trading above $15, reflecting a 30.8% increase over the past five days, according to TipRanks data. 

The massive price surge comes after Khol’s reported its best sales performance in four years during Q1 2026, helping lift investor confidence in its business. The retailer said its comparable sales declined 1.1%, a major improvement from the 2.8% drop seen in Q1 2025. At the same time, the company’s net sales decreased by 1.7%.  

Kohl’s recorded a quarterly revenue of $3 billion, slightly above Wall Street’s $2.99 billion forecast. The retailer also reported a quarterly loss of 13 cents per share, beating analyst forecasts for a 19-cent loss.

Meanwhile, CEO Michael Bender said the company is “knocking on the door of growth” as it continues to cut expenses. It is also improving its inventory management practices and boosting its balance sheet ahead of the next quarter.

Kohl’s Maintains Full-Year Outlook Despite Economic Pressures

Despite struggling with ongoing low sales, Kohl’s is maintaining its full-year outlook. The retailer expects net sales and comparable sales to range from a 2% decline to flat growth for the year, while adjusted earnings are expected to range from $1 to $1.60 per share.

The company claimed its sales are under pressure due to inflation and high energy prices. As a result, lower- and middle-income consumers, who are the largest part of Kohl’s customer base, are spending less.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: PayPal (PYPL) Makes History With First-Ever Sri Lankan Banking Partner
data.description:
PayPal (PYPL) and Sampath Bank have announced a new partnership to expand global payment access for users in Sri Lanka. Through this deal, Sampath Bank will become the first to enable PayPal account linking and withdrawals. The partnership is designed to make international payments easier and more accessible for Sri Lankans.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Sampath Bank Expands Global Payments Through PayPal Integration

PayPal’s system has been integrated into Sampath Vishwa, an online payment portal offered by Sampath Bank. The new alliance marks a major step in Sri Lanka’s payments space, with Sampath becoming the first bank in the nation to offer this service. 

The partnership is set to expand payment access for users and businesses across the country. Currently, demand for safe international payment systems continues to rise as freelancers, online businesses, exporters, and remote workers expand their presence in global markets. As such, many users need better ways to access cross-border payments and receive foreign income. 

With PayPal now part of Sri Lanka’s online banking system, users can finally receive and transfer money through their local bank accounts. The deal will also help people work more easily in the global online trade market. Furthermore, it supports foreign currency inflows into Sri Lanka as more users engage in remote work and online businesses.

How Sri Lankans Can Access PayPal Via Sampath

The new deal automatically links PayPal to the Sampath Vishwa mobile app, allowing users to connect their accounts and manage foreign payments more easily. Funds can be withdrawn directly into LKR or USD accounts, depending on the user’s choice. Those who do not have an existing PayPal account can also set one up in the app, making it easier to get started. 

The service is designed for freelancers, SMEs, exporters, and online sellers who rely on global payment flows. It makes payments easier and helps improve Sri Lanka’s online banking by adding more ways to send and receive foreign money. This, in turn, helps the bank stand out more as it grows its digital financial services.

Sanjaya Gunawardana, Managing Director and CEO of Sampath Bank, described the new deal with PayPal as a step toward opening up global opportunities for Sri Lankans. He added that people should not be held back from taking part in the global economy just because of access limitations.

Is PayPal Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

According to TipRanks analysts, PayPal (PYPL) is rated a Hold. The stock has a 12-month projected price target of $48.71, implying an 11% upside. Out of 28 Wall Street analysts, 3 rate it a Buy, 22 a Hold, and 3 a Sell. Investors can track PYPL’s latest ratings and price targets via the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Cerebras (CBRS) vs Nvidia (NVDA) — Which AI Stock Is a Better Buy Now?
data.description:
Cerebras Systems (CBRS) had a mega public market debut, with its stock rising sharply on its first day of trading. This shows strong investor interest in new artificial intelligence (AI) firms entering the stock market. It also highlights growing attention to new public players in the chipmaker space as the sector expands quickly.

Meet Samuel – Your Personal Investing Prophet

Two ETFs to trade CBRS long or short: CBRX & CBRZ

At the same time, Nvidia (NVDA) remains the leading company in AI chips, although newer firms like Cerebras are seeing major demand. This creates a clearer picture of rising competition in the AI chip space, as investors look at both established leaders.

Cerebras Gains Early Attention With Strong IPO Debut

Cerebras Systems publicly launched on May 14, 2026, and its stock rose 68% on its first day of trading. The strong debut shows heavy investor interest in new AI chip companies entering public markets.

The company uses a wafer-scale chip design that treats an entire silicon wafer as one unit. This approach focuses on boosting processing capacity and improving performance for large AI workloads. 

Cerebras is still in an early growth stage, but investor attention is rising as demand for AI infrastructure continues to expand. The company is also positioning itself as a new public entrant in the fast-growing, competitive AI chip space.

Nvidia Maintains Leadership in AI Chip Demand

Nvidia still leads the AI chip market by a landslide, controlling about 86% of the AI data center space. Strong demand from major tech firms continues to support its top position.

Its systems are also designed to operate flexibly, allowing smaller chips to be grouped together for different tasks. This makes Nvidia’s technology widely usable for both large and small companies. 

Another key strength of Nvidia is CUDA, its long-standing software ecosystem that supports a large developer community. This system makes it difficult for companies to shift demand away from Nvidia, allowing the firm to maintain its lead over other AI chip rivals.

Cerebras vs Nvidia: Which Stock Looks Stronger Right Now

Cerebras offers early-stage growth potential, supported by strong IPO interest and a different chip design approach. However, it is still building long-term market strength, with the CBRS stock currently trading at $256.78 per share after its public debut.

Nvidia, on the other hand, already holds a dominant position, a large ecosystem, and wide industry adoption. Its leadership is supported by its size and well-established tech infrastructure. Presently, NVDA is trading at $215.53 per share. Even though Cerebras trades at a higher price, NVDA has a proven track record and broader market adoption.

Based on current position, stability, and adoption, NVDa is rated a Strong Buy by Wall Street analysts tracked by TipRanks. However, given CBRS’s early entry into the market, its growth outlook is still closely watched. 

Is Nvidia Stock a Good Buy Now?

According to TipRanks’ analysts, Nvidia (NVDA) is rated a Strong Buy, with an average price target of $301, showing a 49% upside. This suggests analysts still expect strong growth potential, even as competition in AI chips intensifies. Investors can track NVDA’s latest ratings and price targets via the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Is Losing the AI Coding War Thanks to GitHub’s Unending Outages
data.description:
GitHub, Microsoft’s (MSFT) developer platform, is facing a growing reliability crisis as demand for artificial intelligence (AI) coding pushes its systems to the limit. This demand has caused the platform to suffer multiple outages over the past few years, frustrating many users. Now, as Microsoft slowly loses its ground, competition from newer AI coding platforms is challenging the tech giant’s early lead in the sector. 

Meet Samuel – Your Personal Investing Prophet

Explore AMZO for 2X short leverage on AMZN

GitHub Outages Strain Systems and Raise User Concerns

Between May 2025 and April 2026, GitHub recorded 257 separate incidents, 48 of which were major outages. Since March this year, the platform has experienced more than a dozen outages, lasting over an hour. 

The surge in AI-assisted coding has been the primary driver behind these challenges. As more developers adopted AI tools to write and review code, the load on GitHub grew faster than the platform could handle, exposing weaknesses in the system. 

A major factor behind the instability is GitHub’s slow and incomplete migration to Azure, Microsoft’s cloud computing platform. Currently, only part of GitHub’s traffic has been moved to Azure data centers, while its older systems are still under heavy workload. This split setup has struggled under the extra workload, contributing to GitHub’s recurring downtime.

Developers and businesses have also voiced their frustration about the issue. Some warned that GitHub is becoming unreliable for serious production work. In addition to outages, the platform has faced security incidents and leadership changes after the exit of its former CEO. Together, these problems have added more pressure on its engineering teams and raised questions about GitHub’s stability. 

Microsoft Faces Rising Competition in AI Coding Space 

Microsoft’s GitHub Copilot has been leading the AI-assisted coding sector for years, but the competition has shifted rapidly recently. Tools like Cursor, built by Anysphere, a software firm, and Claude Code, made by Anthropic, an AI company, are now catching up, as more developers and businesses use them.

According to reports, Cursor has already overtaken Copilot in usage among some developer groups. At the same time, other coding platforms from firms like ChatGPT creator OpenAI, GitLab (GTLB), Amazon (AMZN), and Atlassian (TEAM) are also gaining investors’ interest. These rivals benefit from faster development cycles and stronger adoption of AI-native coding workflows.

Despite the heated competition, GitHub remains one of the largest developer platforms globally, with over 180 million users and a strong base in AI coding. However, increasing preference for newer AI tools is putting pressure on Microsoft to strengthen its Copilot’s capabilities.

Should I Buy Microsoft Stock Now?

Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks rate Microsoft (MSFT) a Strong Buy. The stock currently trades around $419, with a 12-month projected average price target of $559.98 and 33.80% upside potential. Out of 35 analysts, 33 rate it a Buy, 2 a Hold, and 0 a Sell. For more information on the stock’s performance, price target, and rating, visit the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke
